item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good character building — I'm disappointed that I didn't see this until now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was my question too. I think it's the latter. Or maybe that he's hoping it will be a self-fulfilling prophecy - if dragons require wonder to exist, O5-2 is creating new wonder which, he hopes, will create the dragons.

We made it! Thanks to everyone whose read this, I'm glad you've all enjoyed it.

I wonder if we get enough upvotes we can bring dragons back in real life? :thinking:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a question but, was there a Reason you didn't use past tense for the third one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was glad to see that this didn't fall into the trope of using dialogue with the anomaly to allow it to explain plot points the author couldn't figure out how to insert more gracefully. Frankly, I was fully prepared for this to be too cheesy for me, and it surprised me by turning out to be a charming story with just enough of an emotional touch. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im hoping there will be another update or one about the dragons next of kin

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did you do this to me I just wanted a good story now I'm sad.

I always thought good stories are supposed to make you sad :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My heart sank as soon as I saw:

Object Class: Euclid Neutralized

Why did you do this to me I just wanted a good story now I'm sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was easily the best damn dragon-related SCP that I've ever read. I loved the dialogue between 3844 and Dr. Fegmont, and I loved the small changes that became apparent with each iteration of the article. I especially appreciated the personality shift in Dr. Fegmont himself, as he slowly morphed from a young scientist who just found a literal fucking dragon into a cold and calculating member of the O5 Council. The shift in personality parallels the decline of 3844 himself, which is beautiful writing in my opinion.

My only regret is that I have but one upvote to give for this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I’ll just stick with the traditional 4 legs :P

Good, I hate it when dragons are depicted with only 2 legs. *Glares in the general direction of Skyrim*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, this was a lovely read! You really managed to make dragons work in a refreshing way, along with deliver a rather tragic tale. Poor dragon, he really hoped to be free yet again, believing the foundation will grant that one day. I grew worried for him as soon as the description kept updating to showing that he’s only getting smaller and less vibrant. I like how you showed the updates throughout the years, I thought that was a clever way to present the story.

+1 for sure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like most good math text books, I prefer to leave this as an exercise for the reader.

Or, in other words, I don’t know. Do you believe in dragons?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, this was a wonderful read. I have a question though, did O5-2 actually find more dragons or does the final iteration of the article just mean that he "believes" in them again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m glad you enjoyed!

Honestly, one of my bigger fears about this was being too similar to 1762, but I distance myself enough.

(Also that’s the one other skip I know of that features some form of “dragon”)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really reminds me of SCP-1762, in a good way. The gradual fading of the dragon is immensely sad, but the ending is like a happy ending to it all. Even if people stop believing in one area, there are still others that believe in different ways, in different areas. It's a hopeful message. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay! I’m glad you got your ticket to feels station :P

As for the legs…

four
two
seventeen
one and three quarters
all the legs

I think I’ll just stick with the traditional 4 legs :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd actually been toying with the idea of a dragon SCP that was contained via diplomacy ever since noticing the distressing lack of actual dragons on this site. Did you use time travel to plagiarize my future self?

Joking aside, I loved this. Dragons are my favorite mythical creatures, so I'm glad to finally see an SCP about a proper dragon, let alone a well-written one that took me on a feels trip.

Important question: how many legs does he have?

Innocence is lost
When we know better
So close your eyes and dream
Let's believe together

Thanks everyone for reading! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this. It plays out nicely and doesn't get bogged down in cliched tropes around dragons too much. My only real issue with it is the whole "guy from the start of the story becomes an O5 member", which is getting a bit overdone (or may already be!). The fact that he then continues to be actively involved in the containment of specific stuff seems a bit of a stretch and far beyond the purview of the O5.

Still, those are minor head-canony gripes and don't really detract from anything, so +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't going to upvote due to the cliche-ness of the article until the final iteration. The final iteration bucked the cliche enough for me to upvote.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Tufto, I’m happy I’m going out of Series IV with a bang. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tried to think of one more iteration to put between the 3rd and 4th iterations, but nothing that came to mind would have made a meaningful enough contribution to the story to be worth the space.

Well, if you don't think you could have put another in then I suppose it's better to leave it as it is than try and force one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love everything about this. It's such a sad, sweet story, with the slow fading of the dragon as a world of magic dies. And then that wonderful, vindictive sting in the tail :D.

Very easy +1, one of the best of Series 4.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you enjoyed!

Each version was kinda short, and while the length worked for each one individually, I don't think it would hurt to have put more versions in

See, when I sit down to write an iterations based format screw, I try hard to make sure each iteration tries to tell its own miniature story that also fits into the broader picture. I tried to think of one more iteration to put between the 3rd and 4th iterations, but nothing that came to mind would have made a meaningful enough contribution to the story to be worth the space. Also, it seems that at this length people have adequate time to get attached to our scaly friend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Truly heartbreaking. You made me choke up a little when I realized what was happening; first article I've read that has ever elicited this level of emotion from me.

The ray of hope at the end is uplifting, but I would've loved it the same either way. +1 Forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nooooooooo……

Why is every story I've seen about dragons on this site sad? Although I suppose that would make the most sense.

But there are more… and maybe there are dragons in space…

But in all seriousness, this was a great scip. I can really empathize with the dragon, and I like the way he was designed. The way he fades out is sad, as humanity loses wonder, but he still shows strength at the end. In fact, the whole document itself is another sad reminder of the inevitable consequences of the Foundation's work; that they sometimes lock up friendly things, and things of childhood wonder.
I like the format of clicking through old versions of the document to the current one, as I've seen in other scips, giving a timeline of events. There's also the blue text so you can compare changes easily, which is nice.

However, I do feel it could have been a bit longer. Each version was kinda short, and while the length worked for each one individually, I don't think it would hurt to have put more versions in. But other than that, +1.

Man, I wish I had inspiration for my own dragon scip…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is probably one of my new just favorite comments in general

/thumbsup

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A beautiful but sad article

take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3844 is the collective designation for numerous, large, winged lizards

Yes, insult the Keter dragons. That will make them easier to contain. /sarcasm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just want to cry.
He was so damn nice, too!
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
o no.

I've been spelling it wrong this entire time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I looked up some clips of Paarathurnax, and that makes sense why you're reminded of him. In my head the dragon sounded like, distinctly british. Not like, a super heavy british accent, but a noticeable one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No references here. I just imagine the dragon here speaking with the same guttural voice as they do in the game. Also, this dragon's personality sorta reminds of …..

+ spoilers and lore
- collapse 

Paarathurnax. Probably because it was the first dragon in the game to become an ally. Paarathurnax is also the brother of the main antagonist and tells you what you need to defeat him. A long time before the game takes place, he was a lieutenant of his brother's army, witnessing and commiting various atrocities on mankind. Even though he isn't anymore while trying to atone for his crimes, this could create a rift between you and a group of dragon slayers you meet earlier in the game. They want Paarathurnax to pay for his crimes with his, while you have the option of agreeing to help them or disagreeing to help them.


It's an interesting game and I definitely recommend it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THE DARGON

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did I do an accidental Skyrim reference? Idk I never played the games…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was told that I should make the name of the dragons some sort of pun or elongation of "puff" but I couldn't think of any that sounded good to me :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Now dragons live forever/
Oh, wait now, no they don't
Our scaly guy's shot from the skies/
'Cause he flew when we said don't."

I was quite affected by the ending, but reassured that this wasn't the last/only dragon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed! I'm sure there's another way you could probably write a dragon-centric skip though, so don't give up on that just yet! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just finished Skyrim which makes the interview logs much more enjoyable to read.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who loves dragons, I was a bit sad I didn't get to write the SCP over dragons myself. However this article was brilliant, I'm glad a creature with as much lore and depth to it throughout history got such a fitting article. The story about the dragon itself was very sad as well, one of the few skips you really get to know for itself rather than just what the foundations description of it says.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
\o/

I love how the historical bits tell a vivid story of how the Foundation and the world change, both together and in reaction to each other.

Honestly, I am kind of kicking myself that I thought of this after I posted my 4K. I like my 4K, but I feel like this plays to the history theme more nicely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Take my +1 you monster, I haven't felt so attached to a character in an SCP character in a long time and then you pull the "author giveth, author taketh away" on us

/weeps quietly in a corner

This is a lovely read, and I love how the historical bits tell a vivid story of how the Foundation and the world change, both together and in reaction to each other. It was a pleasure to read this in the draft stage and I'm glad to see it on the mainsite!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you enjoyed! I was personally a little surprised that I couldn't find an SCP just involving like, a straight up dragon. We have some dangerous lizards, and I know there's one article that has oragami dragons, but no straight up, regular dragons.

At least not that I found.

Queue someone linking like 4 dragon skips that I missed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is how you write an SCP article with dragons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you enjoyed!

But I'm very glad it didn't just end with "dragon fades away because people stop believing in magic".

That would be have been just a little trite, despite the emotional impact.

Yeah, when I was first playing around with this idea, I was thinking of going full fade, but then I realized that like… That's a general structure/trend that is trodden quite often on this site. I'm happy the twist helped!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intensely sad, but with a real surprise ending.

The initial page drew me in with the period-style writing and the format screw. Then it was neat seeing what WW2 did to the setup. Then the changes came in and oh boy, that just about killed me. But I'm very glad it didn't just end with "dragon fades away because people stop believing in magic". That would be have been just a little trite, despite the emotional impact. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beautiful.

item.title: Alternate Title - How To Contain Your Dragon
item.content:encoded:
The words he speaks
The fire he breathes
Helps me remember
The world still has whimsy

Suggested Music

And with this, I say goodbye to Series IV, and say hello to another 1000 SCPs.

Thanks to my critiquers: Bardeen, incidental_cabbage, fieldstone, Cyantreuse, Hippo, Croquembouche, Researcher Blasko, Sir Baubius, ihp and Zyn (Also stormbreath crit too).

Also the code for the very last iteration is stolen from not_a_seagull's SCP-3095.

If you want to read more of my articles, you can find them here
